project_type: TypeScript Vite + React frontend with WebSocket server for a board game
build_system: npm with Vite for frontend, tsx for server
test_framework: No formal test framework. Validation via build.
verification_instructions: Run npm run build to confirm TypeScript compiles without errors
environment_constraints: Full internet access available. No Docker used.
key_patterns: Game state flows through GameState interface (src/types/game.ts). Logic in gameLogic.ts exports pure functions. Store in gameStore.ts uses Zustand with WebSocket for online mode. Server in src/server/index.ts handles socket events and broadcasts state. Interactive popups use patterns: (1) state flag like zorroPlacementActive + playerId + remaining count, (2) UI state in store like warPhasePopupVisible, (3) ready-player arrays like warPhaseReadyPlayers for multi-player acknowledgment, (4) server case handlers that accumulate readyPlayers then transition state when all players ready.
directory_structure: src/types/game.ts (types + card data + provinces), src/utils/gameLogic.ts (4848 lines, all game logic), src/store/gameStore.ts (3966 lines, Zustand store + UI state), src/server/index.ts (2414 lines, WebSocket server), src/components/ (React components), src/i18n/ (translations en.ts/es.ts)
